meta_001: Multiple Intelligence Test — My map of 8 intelligences
meta_002: One IQ score can’t sum you up. Map your mind with Gardner’s 8 intelligences.
html_004: One IQ score can’t sum you up.
html_008: and everyone carries a map that’s all their own.
html_012: When the 24 questions are done, we’ll even reveal
html_013: the activities that match your top intelligence combo
html_016:
※ A self-report reference test that borrows the categories from Harvard psychologist Howard Gardner’s theory of Multiple Intelligences (1983, 『Frames of Mind』).
    It doesn’t measure ability — it’s closer to a
html_019: Eight intelligence pillars are rising — which one is which? Revealed at the end!
html_020: 👉 How much is this like you?
html_023:
— Howard Gardner, 『Frames of Mind: The Theory of Multiple Intelligences』(1983).
    His idea that “intelligence isn’t one thing but many, and everyone’s mix is different” — this test borrows those 8 categories to observe your tendencies.
html_026: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_001: I enjoy wordplay and puns, and I save sentences I love.
js_002: When I explain complicated things out loud or in writing, people actually get it.
js_003: I remember new words and phrases I hear, and I put them to use.
js_004: Patterns in numbers or odd statistics jump out at me.
js_005: I love asking ‘why is that?’ and tracing cause and effect.
js_006: Organizing things into planners, budgets, or spreadsheets is secretly satisfying.
js_007: Once I’ve been somewhere, I can find my way back without a map.
js_008: I can rearrange furniture or rotate an object entirely in my head.
js_009: People have told me I’ve got an eye for art, photos, or design.
js_010: After hearing a song once or twice, I can hum the melody back.
js_011: When something’s off-pitch or off-beat, it grates on my ears right away.
js_012: I’ve got playlists split up by mood.
js_013: I learn faster by diving in with my body than by thinking (sports, cooking, assembling).
js_014: Sitting still too long makes me antsy.
js_015: I’m confident building and fixing things with my hands.
js_016: When friends fall out, I tend to be the one who patches it up.
js_017: People say I get comfortable with someone I’ve just met really fast.
js_018: I’m quick to notice shifts in other people’s moods.
js_019: I absolutely need quiet time alone to sort out my thoughts.
js_020: I tend to put my feelings and thoughts down in a journal or notes.
js_021: I can describe my own strengths and weaknesses pretty specifically.
js_022: I enjoy observing or caring for animals and plants.
js_023: I feel shifts in the seasons and weather more keenly than most.
js_024: Mountains, the sea, or a forest genuinely recharge me.

js_033: Design, photo & video, interiors, map-free travel
js_035: the power to read sound and rhythm
js_036: Instruments, playlist curation, composing, live shows
js_038: the power to think with your body
js_039: Sports & dance, cooking, woodworking & DIY, hands-on hobbies
js_041: the power to read people and connect them
js_042: Running groups, mentoring, counseling & mediation, leading teams
js_044: the power to observe and handle yourself
js_045: Journaling & reflection, meditation, solo projects, career planning
js_047: the power to sense nature and life
js_048: Gardening, hiking & camping, pets, environmental causes
